title: UGC NET Previous Year Questions
text: Welcome to the UGC NET Previous Year Questions module of Geography OpenCourseWare. This page is completely aligned with the official UGC NET Geography (Code No. 80) syllabus. Below is the detailed breakdown of all 10 units along with their key focus areas for PYQ preparation.

title: UGC NET Previous Year Questions
text:
Unit I: Geomorphology






NoteKey Topics




Tectonics & Forces: Continental Drift, Plate Tectonics, Endogenetic and Exogenetic forces.
Denudation: Weathering and Denudation processes.
Geomorphic Cycles: Models of Davis and Penck.
Slope Development: Theories and processes.
Earth Movements: Seismicity, folding, faulting, and vulcanicity.
Hazards: Earthquakes, volcanoes, landslides, and avalanches.

title: UGC NET Previous Year Questions
text:
Unit II: Climatology






TipKey Topics




Atmosphere: Composition and structure, Insolation, Heat Budget.
Temperature & Winds: Pressure, Winds, and General Atmospheric Circulation (air-masses, fronts, cyclones, anticyclones).
Climate Classification: Models by Köppen and Thornthwaite.
ENSO: El Niño, La Niña, and Southern Oscillations.
Hazards: Cyclones, Thunderstorms, Tornadoes, Hailstorms, Heat/Cold waves, Drought, Cloudburst, GLOF.
Climate Change: Evidences, causes in the past, and human impact on global climate.

title: UGC NET Previous Year Questions
text:
Unit III: Oceanography






ImportantKey Topics




Basics: Relief of Oceans, Composition (Temperature, Density, Salinity).
Circulation: Warm and Cold Currents, Waves, and Tides.
Sea Level: Sea Level Changes.
Hazards: Tsunami and Cyclone impacts.

title: UGC NET Previous Year Questions
text:
Unit IV: Geography of Environment






NoteKey Topics




Ecosystems: Geographic Classification, Human Ecology, Trophic Levels, Energy Flows.
Biogeochemical Cycles: Carbon, nitrogen, and oxygen cycles, Food Chains/Webs, Ecological Pyramids.
Human Impacts: Environmental Ethics, Deep Ecology.
Hazards: Global Warming, Urban Heat Island, Pollution (Atmospheric, Water), Land Degradation.
Policies: National Programmes, Legal Framework, International Treaties (Brundtland Commission, Kyoto Protocol, Agenda 21, SDGs, Paris Agreement).

title: UGC NET Previous Year Questions
section: Unit V: Population and Settlement Geography
text:
Unit V: Population and Settlement Geography






TipKey Topics




Population Geography: Data sources, Distribution patterns, Growth globally. Theories (Malthus, Sadler, Ricardo), Demographic Transition, Fertility, Mortality, Migration. Population Composition and Policies globally.
Settlement Geography: Rural Settlements (types, patterns, contemporary problems). Origin of Towns (Childe, Pirenne, Mumford). Urbanization processes, Urban Systems (Primate city, Rank-size rule), Central Place Theories (Christaller, Losch).
Urban Structure: Models by Burgess, Harris, Ullman, Hoyt. Megacities, Global Cities, Edge Cities. Manifestation of Urban Poverty.

title: UGC NET Previous Year Questions
section: Unit VI: Geography of Economic Activities and Regional Development
text:
Unit VI: Geography of Economic Activities and Regional Development






ImportantKey Topics




Economic Geography: Factors of spatial organization (primary, secondary, tertiary, quaternary), Natural Resources, World Energy Crises.
Agricultural Geography: Land Use Planning, Cop combination (Weaver, Doi, Rafiullah), Von Thunen’s Model, Agricultural Systems.
Industrial Geography: Location theories (Weber, Hoover, Losch, Pred, Smith), Globalisation impacts, ICT and Knowledge Industries.
Transport and Trade: Spatial interaction models (Ullman, Hurst), Gravity Model, WTO, Globalisation, Regional Cooperation.
Regional Development: Typologies of regions, Disparities, Theories (Hirschman, Myrdal, Friedman), Dependency Theory.

title: UGC NET Previous Year Questions
section: Unit VII: Cultural, Social and Political Geography
text:
Unit VII: Cultural, Social and Political Geography






NoteKey Topics




Cultural and Social: Concept of Culture, Cultural Ecology/Convergence. Social Structure, Well-being, Exclusion. Spatial distribution of social groups in India (Tribe, Caste, Religion, Language). Diseases Ecology, Health Care Planning.
Political Geography: Boundaries and Frontiers, Heartland and Rimland Theories. Federalism, Electoral Reforms, and Electoral Behaviour in India. Geopolitics of Climate Change and Resources, Regional Organisations (SAARC, ASEAN, OPEC, EU).

title: UGC NET Previous Year Questions
text:
Unit VIII: Geographic Thought






TipKey Topics




Scholars & Contributions: Greek, Roman, Arab, Chinese, and Indian scholars. Geographers like Varenius, Kant, Humboldt, Ritter, Schaefer, Hartshorne.
Trends & Traditions: Darwinian Theory impact, Indian Geography trends, Earth Science, Area Studies, Spatial Analysis.
Dualisms: Physical vs. Human, Regional vs. Systematic, Qualitative vs. Quantitative, Ideographic vs. Nomothetic.
Paradigms & Perspectives: Positivism, Behaviouralism, Humanism, Structuralism, Feminism, Postmodernism.

title: UGC NET Previous Year Questions
text:
Unit IX: Geographical Techniques






ImportantKey Topics




Cartography & Mapping: Sources of data, Map Types, Map Making Techniques (Choropleth, Isarithmic, Flow Maps), Pie Diagrams.
GIS & Remote Sensing: Raster/Vector formats, DEM, Georeferencing, Spatial decision support. Electromagnetic Spectrum, Sensors, Aerial Photographs, Digital Image Processing. GPS.
Statistical Methods: Central Tendency, Dispersion, Inequalities, Sampling, Hypothesis Testing (Chi Square, T-test, ANOVA), Correlation, Regression, Principal Component Analysis, Cluster Analysis.
Morphometric Analysis: Stream Ordering, Bifurcation Ratio, Drainage Density, Basin Circularity, Slope Analysis.

title: UGC NET Previous Year Questions
text:
Unit X: Geography of India






NoteKey Topics




Physical Profile: Physiographic Regions, Drainage, Indian Monsoon, Jet Streams, Himalayan Cryosphere.
Resources: Soil, Vegetation, Water, Mineral, Marine.
Population: Distribution, Growth, Composition, Population Policies.
Agriculture & Industry: Major food crops, Agro-Climatic Zones, Green Revolution, Food Security, Industrial Policies since Independence.
Economy & Transport: Transport Networks, Internal/External Trade, Regional Development Planning, Impact of Globalisation.
Hazards: Earthquakes, Droughts, Floods, Cyclones, Tsunami, Himalayan Disasters.





Books and Further Readings
Focus your preparation on practicing questions corresponding to these standard 10 units. Use the modules across Geography OpenCourseWare to master each topic!

title: Teaching Learning Methodology
text:
Geography is not just about locations; it is about the relationships between people, places, and the environment. Our teaching methodology is designed to translate complex theoretical concepts into practical, spatial insights through four core pillars.


Core Pillars of Our Instruction


 Self-Paced Learning
Flexible access to curated digital content, high-quality video modules, and structured reading materials, allowing students to master geography at their own speed.
 Visit YouTube Channel


 Classroom Discussion & Doubt Solving
Interactive, direct engagement where theoretical complexities are simplified, and student doubts are addressed through expert-led dialogue and real-time feedback.


 Student Interaction & Peer Discussion
A collaborative learning environment that encourages peer-to-peer knowledge sharing, group analysis of spatial data, and diverse geographical perspectives.





Our Pedagogical Approach

Blended Learning: A combination of high-quality video modules, interactive reading materials, and hands-on spatial exercises.
Micro-Learning Units: Complex topics are broken down into digestible fragments, ensuring better retention and easier mastery of specific curriculum points.
Syllabus Alignment: Every unit is precisely mapped to national academic standards (such as UGC NET) to ensure direct utility for exam preparation.
Visual Excellence: We believe geography is inherently visual. Our materials feature premium 3D visualizations, technical diagrams, and high-resolution maps.




Student Outcomes

Critical Analysis: Ability to evaluate environmental and human interactions critically.
Technical Proficiency: Mastery of modern geographical techniques and statistical tools.
Research Readiness: Foundational knowledge required to conduct independent, high-quality geographical research.


“The study of geography is more than just memorizing places on a map. It’s about understanding the complexity of our world, appreciating the diversity of cultures that exists across continents. And in the end, it’s about using all that knowledge to help bridge divides and bring people together.” — Barack Obama

title: Social & Cultural Geography
text: Welcome to the Social & Cultural Geography module of Geography OpenCourseWare. This course explores how culture, identity, religion, ethnicity, and social structures are distributed across space and how they interact with geographic landscapes and processes.

title: Social & Cultural Geography
text:
UGC NET Syllabus

Concept of Culture, Cultural Complexes, Areas and Region, Cultural Heritage, Cultural Ecology, Cultural Convergence.
Social Structure and Processes, Social Well-being and Quality of Life, Social Exclusion.
Spatial distribution of social groups in India (Tribe, Caste, Religion and Language).
Environment and Human Health, Diseases Ecology.
Nutritional Status (etiological conditions, classification and spatial and seasonal distributional patterns with special reference to India).
Health Care Planning and Policies in India, Medical Tourism in India.

title: Regional Development
text: Welcome to the Regional Development module of Geography OpenCourseWare. This course examines the theories and practices of regional planning and development — addressing spatial inequalities, planning strategies, and the quest for balanced territorial development.

title: Population Geography
text: Welcome to the Population Geography module of Geography OpenCourseWare. This course studies the spatial dimensions of human population — including distribution, density, growth trends, demographic transitions, migration, and their implications for development.

title: Population Geography
text:
UGC NET Syllabus

Sources of population data (census, sample surveys and vital statistics, data reliability and errors).
World Population Distribution (measures, patterns and determinants), World Population Growth (prehistoric to modern period).
Demographic Transition, Theories of Population Growth (Malthus, Sadler, and Ricardo).
Fertility and Mortality Analysis (indices, determinants and world patterns).
Migration (types, causes and consequences and models).
Population Composition and Characteristics (age, sex, rural-urban, occupational structure and educational levels).
Population Policies in Developed and Developing Countries.

title: Indian Socio-Economic Geography
text: Welcome to the Indian Socio-Economic Geography module of Geography OpenCourseWare. This course provides a comprehensive understanding of the socio-economic landscape of India — including population dynamics, urbanization patterns, industrial development, agricultural change, and regional disparities.

title: Indian Socio-Economic Geography
text:
UGC NET Syllabus

Population Characteristics (spatial patterns of distribution), Growth and Composition, Determinants of Population, Population Policies in India.
Agriculture (Production, Productivity and Yield of Major Food Crops), Major Crop Regions, Regional Variations in Agricultural Development, Environmental, Technological and Institutional Factors affecting Indian Agriculture; Agro-Climatic Zones, Green Revolution, Food Security and Right to Food.
Industrial Development since Independence, Industrial Regions and their characteristics, Industrial Policies in India.
Development and Patterns of Transport Networks (railways, roadways, waterways, airways and pipelines), Internal and External Trade, Regional Development Planning in India.
Globalisation and its impact on Indian Economy.

title: Climatology
text: Welcome to the Climatology module of Geography OpenCourseWare. This course explores the Earth’s atmosphere, climate systems, and the physical processes that drive weather and climate variability at local, regional, and global scales.

title: Climatology
text:
Atmospheric Circulation
The unequal heating of the Earth’s surface creates pressure gradients, giving rise to complex global and regional wind systems that transport heat and moisture across the globe.

Get the Presentation ↗   |   Watch the Video ↗






ImportantWind Systems & Pressure Belts




General Circulation (Tri-Cellular Model): The Hadley Cell drives trade winds near the equator; the Ferrel Cell drives westerlies in the mid-latitudes; and the Polar Cell creates polar easterlies. This three-cell structure explains the primary latitudinal shifting of climate zones.
Pressure Belts: Include the Equatorial Low (Doldrums), Subtropical High (Horse Latitudes), Subpolar Low, and Polar High. Their seasonal migration, following the apparent movement of the sun, dictates wet and dry seasons across the tropics.
Jet Streams: Narrow, high-speed meandering air currents in the upper troposphere. The Subtropical and Polar jet streams significantly influence the paths of surface weather systems and aviation routes.
Monsoons: Massive seasonal reversals of wind direction caused by the differential heating of land and sea. The Indian Monsoon is the most prominent, fundamentally shaping the hydrology, agriculture, and economy of South Asia.
Local Winds: Thermally driven local circulations including land/sea breezes, mountain/valley anabatic and katabatic winds, and regional phenomena like the warm/dry Chinook (Rockies), Foehn (Alps), and the cold Mistral (France).

title: Agricultural Geography
text: Welcome to the Agricultural Geography module of Geography OpenCourseWare. This course examines the spatial organization of agriculture — including farming systems, crop distribution, land use patterns, agricultural revolutions, and contemporary challenges of food security.

title: Economic Geography
text: Welcome to the Economic Geography module of Geography OpenCourseWare. This course explores how economic activities — primary, secondary, tertiary, and quaternary — are distributed across space and how location, resources, and human decisions shape economic landscapes.

title: Economic Geography
text:
UGC NET Syllabus

Factors affecting spatial organisation of economic activities (primary, secondary, tertiary and quarternary).
Natural Resources (classification, distribution and associated problems), Natural Resources Management.
World Energy Crises in Developed and Developing Countries.

title: Geographical Thought
text: Welcome to the Geographical Thought module of Geography OpenCourseWare. This course traces the intellectual history of geography as a discipline — from the earliest map-makers and travelers to modern critical and quantitative paradigms.

title: Geographical Thought
text:
UGC NET Syllabus

Contributions of Greek, Roman, Arab, Chinese and Indian Scholars.
Contributions of Geographers (Bernhardus Varenius, Immanuel Kant, Alexander von Humboldt, Carl Ritter, Scheafer & Hartshorne).
Impact of Darwinian Theory on Geographical Thought.
Contemporary trends in Indian Geography: Cartography, Thematic and Methodological contributions.
Major Geographic Traditions (Earth Science, man-environment relationship, area studies and spatial analysis).
Dualisms in Geographic Studies (physical vs. human, regional vs. systematic, qualitative vs. quantitative, ideographic vs. nomothetic).
Paradigm Shift, Perspectives in Geography (Positivism, Behaviouralism, Humanism, Structuralism, Feminism and Postmodernism).

title: GIS & Remote Sensing
text: Welcome to the GIS & Remote Sensing module of Geography OpenCourseWare. This course covers the principles and applications of Geographic Information Systems and satellite-based remote sensing — the technological backbone of modern geographical analysis.

title: GIS & Remote Sensing
text:
UGC NET Syllabus

GIS Database (raster and vector data formats and attribute data formats), Functions of GIS (conversion, editing and analysis).
Digital Elevation Model (DEM), Georeferencing (coordinate system and map projections and Datum).
GIS Applications (thematic cartography, spatial decision support system).
Basics of Remote Sensing (Electromagnetic Spectrum, Sensors and Platforms, Resolution and Types, Elements of Air Photo and Satellite Image Interpretation and Photogrammetry), Types of Aerial Photographs.
Digital Image Processing: Developments in Remote Sensing Technology and Big Data Sharing and its applications in Natural Resources Management in India.
GPS Components (space, ground control and receiver segments) and Applications.

title: Indian Physical Geography
text: Welcome to the Indian Physical Geography module of Geography OpenCourseWare. This course provides a comprehensive study of India’s physiographic divisions, drainage systems, climate patterns, soils, and natural vegetation — the physical foundation of the subcontinent.

title: Indian Physical Geography
text:
UGC NET Syllabus

Major Physiographic Regions and their Characteristics; Drainage System (Himalayan and Peninsular).
Climate: Seasonal Weather Characteristics, Climatic Divisions, Indian Monsoon (mechanism and characteristics), Jet Streams and Himalayan Cryosphere.
Types and Distribution of Natural Resources: Soil, Vegetation, Water, Mineral and Marine Resources.
Natural Disasters in India (Earthquake, Drought, Flood, Cyclone, Tsunami, Himalayan Highland Hazards and Disasters).

title: Indian Physical Geography
text:
Natural Vegetation
India’s diverse flora.

Get the Presentation ↗   |   Watch the Video ↗






TipVegetation Types




Tropical Evergreen: Dense, multi-layered canopy — Western Ghats, Andaman Islands, Northeast India (>200 cm rainfall).
Tropical Deciduous: India’s most extensive forest type — Teak, Sal, and bamboo-dominated.
Tropical Thorn: Scrub and xerophytic species — Rajasthan, interior Deccan.
Montane Forests: Successional vegetation from subtropical broadleaf to alpine meadows.
Mangroves: Sundarbans, Bhitarkanika, and Gujarat’s coastal mangrove systems.
Biodiversity Hotspots: Western Ghats and Eastern Himalayas.

title: Industrial Geography
text: Welcome to the Industrial Geography module of Geography OpenCourseWare. This course examines the spatial distribution of industries, the factors influencing industrial location, theories of industrial geography, and the changing nature of manufacturing in the global economy.

title: Industrial Geography
text:
UGC NET Syllabus

Classification of Industries, Factors of Industrial Location; Theories of Industrial Location (A. Weber, E. M. Hoover, August Losch, A. Pred and D. M. Smith).
World Industrial Regions, Impact of Globalisation on manufacturing sector in Less Developed Countries.
Tourism Industry.
World distribution and growth of Information And Communication Technology (ICT) and Knowledge Production (Education and R & D) Industries.

title: Political Geography
text: Welcome to the Political Geography module of Geography OpenCourseWare. This course examines how political power is organized spatially — from the formation of nation-states and boundary disputes to contemporary geopolitical rivalries and electoral geography.

title: Political Geography
text:
UGC NET Syllabus

Boundaries and Frontiers (with special reference to India).
Heartland and Rimland Theories.
Trends and Developments in Political Geography.
Geography of Federalism, Electoral Reforms in India, Determinants of Electoral Behaviour.
Geopolitics of Climate Change, Geopolitics of World Resources, Geo-politics of India Ocean.
Regional Organisations of Cooperation (SAARC, ASEAN, OPEC, EU), Neopolitics of World Natural Resources.

title: Reading Materials Sources
text:
Welcome to our curated collection of reading materials. These sources provide high-quality textbooks, research papers, and interactive learning modules from leading academic networks.


E-learning Portals
To support your self-study and provide access to authoritative resources, we recommend the following platforms:


 #### e-Adhyayan (e-Books) High-quality e-Books for postgraduate Geography courses provided by INFLIBNET.


 #### e-PG Pathshala Comprehensive curriculum-based interactive content and video lectures.


 #### NPTEL Courses Online courses in Geography and related disciplines provided by India’s top technical institutes.





How to use these materials

e-Adhyayan: Best for looking up standard university textbook material.
e-PG Pathshala: Provides modular self-paced learning content and video explanations.
NPTEL Courses: Excellent for in-depth academic lectures and structured certifications from premier institutions.

title: Settlement Geography
text: Welcome to the Settlement Geography module of Geography OpenCourseWare. This course examines the origin, growth, classification, and spatial arrangement of human settlements — from small rural hamlets to sprawling megacities.

title: Settlement Geography
text:
UGC NET Syllabus

Rural Settlements (types, patterns and distribution), Contemporary Problems of Rural Settlements (rural-urban migration; land use changes; land acquisition and transactions).
Theories of Origin of Towns (Gordon Childe, Henri Pirenne, Lewis Mumford).
Characteristics and Processes of Urbanization in Developed and Developing Countries.
Urban Systems (the law of the primate city and rank size rule), Central Place Theories (Christaller and Losch).
Internal Structure of the City, Models of Urban Land Use (Burgess, Harris and Ullman, and Hoyt).
Concepts of Megacities, Global Cities and Edge Cities, Changing Urban Forms, Social Segregation in the City, Urban Social Area Analysis, Manifestation of Poverty in the City (slums, informal sector growth, crime and social exclusion).

title: Statistical Geography
text: Welcome to the Statistical Geography module of Geography OpenCourseWare. This course covers the quantitative and statistical techniques essential for rigorous geographic research — from basic descriptive statistics to advanced spatial modeling.

title: Statistical Geography
text:
UGC NET Syllabus

Sources of Geographic Information and Data (spatial and non-spatial).
Types of Maps, Techniques of Map Making (Choropleth, Isarithmic, Dasymetric, Chorochromatic, Flow Maps) Data Representation on Maps (Pie diagrams, Bar diagrams and Line Graph).
Applications of Measures of Central Tendency, Dispersion and Inequalities, Sampling, Sampling Procedure and Hypothesis Testing (chi square test, t test, ANOVA).
Time Series Analysis, Correlation and Regression Analysis.
Measurement of Indices, Making Indicators Scale Free, Computation of Composite Index, Principal Component Analysis and Cluster Analysis.
Morphometric Analysis: Ordering of Streams, Bifurcation Ratio, Drainage Density and Drainage Frequency, Basin Circularity Ratio and Form Factor, Profiles, Slope Analysis, Clinographic Curve, Hypsographic Curve and Altimetric Frequency Graph.

title: Trade and Transport
text: Welcome to the Trade and Transport module of Geography OpenCourseWare. This course examines the spatial patterns of trade and the transportation systems that connect producers to consumers across local, national, and global scales.

title: Trade and Transport
text:
UGC NET Syllabus

Theories and Models of spatial interaction (Edward Ullman and M. E. Hurst).
Measures and Indices of connectivity and accessibility.
Spatial Flow Models: Gravity Model and its variants.
World Trade Organisation, Globalisation and Liberalisation and World Trade Patterns.
Problems and Prospects of Inter and Intra Regional Cooperation and Trade.
